summary: Iteration 2 testing complete. 42/42 backend pytest cases passing (30 regression from iter1 + 12 new for uploads/aisles/atomic-concurrency/checkout-resilience). All new features verified end-to-end. Frontend critical paths confirmed: ImageUploader present on Create Event, aisles rendered as gaps on attendee seat map, booking → /checkout/{id} shows countdown timer + Pay with Stripe button, profile loads bookings. Stripe 500 from iter1 is FIXED (now returns last-known DB state on Stripe API failure).

issues: DATE & TIME field uses browser-default datetime-local picker (input[type=datetime-local] rendered as mm/dd/yyyy, --:-- --) instead of a shadcn Calendar component. Inconsistent with the rest of the dark/premium UI theme.

Could not locate the seatmap-enable toggle via 'button[role=switch]' or generic checkbox selectors during automated testing. Toggle is presumably present (designer renders when enabled per code review), but using a less standard element. Consider adding data-testid='seatmap-toggle' for testability.

action_items: (Optional/minor) Add data-testid='seatmap-toggle' to the has_seatmap switch in CreateEvent.jsx for easier automated testing.
(Optional/design) Replace the native datetime-local picker on Create Event with the shadcn Calendar + time picker for visual consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1150 lines — strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth, events, bookings, payments, uploads, admin). Maintainability concern.

POST /api/uploads stores files on local filesystem (/app/backend/uploads). On container restart or scale-out, files are lost / not shared. For prod use S3/GCS or a persistent volume. Also no virus/MIME sniffing — extension whitelist alone is a weak check; consider validating actual image magic bytes via Pillow.open().

create_hold inserts seat reservations one-by-one in a Python for-loop. If 5 of 6 seats succeed and the 6th hits DuplicateKeyError, we roll back manually. This is correct but not transactional. For larger seat selections, consider Mongo multi-document transactions or batch insert_many with ordered=True.

checkout_status now has try/except — good fix. But it silently returns the old status on Stripe failures; consider logging at WARN with session_id (currently does) and adding a 'stripe_unreachable' flag in response so the frontend can show a hint to the user.

ALLOWED_IMAGE_EXTS includes '.gif' — animated gifs can be large; size limit (5MB) covers it but the gif may not render correctly as a seat-map backdrop. Consider restricting backdrops to png/jpg/webp only.
Demo seed_admin still not idempotent on password change — flagged in iter1, not addressed. Low priority for MVP.
test_credentials: admin@aura.events/admin123, organizer@aura.events/organizer123, attendee@aura.events/attendee123 — all seeded and verified working
seed_data_creation: No new seed needed. Backend startup seeds 3 users + 8 events. Seeded 'Stand-Up Saturday: The Roast' has 16 aisles, 'Hamilton' has 20 aisles — verified via GET /events/{id}. Iter2 tests create transient TEST_-prefixed events and unique-id seat holds; no DB cleanup required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files: test_aura_backend.py (30 cases, iter1 regression) and test_iteration2.py (12 cases, iter2 new). Both at /app/backend/tests/. Run together: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Atomic seat hold concurrency test now strictly enforces [200,409] thanks to unique compound index (event_id, seat_id). Aisle rejection returns 400 with 'aisles' in message. Upload endpoint: 401 unauth / 403 attendee / 400 bad-ext / 200 organizer/admin. Frontend testids confirmed: cover-uploader-btn, cover-uploader-input, seat-{R}-{C} (aisles excluded from DOM). Stripe checkout polling is now resilient.
rca of the issue: N/A — no unresolved issues. Iter1's Stripe 500 was fixed by wrapping stripe.get_checkout_status() in try/except and returning last-known DB state. Verified via test_iteration2.py::TestCheckoutResilient::test_checkout_session_and_status_no_500.
